headline: Hormuz Fears Set a Tense Tone for Tuesday's Open
body_html:
Tuesday's pre-market arrives under a cloud of geopolitical tension after the United States confirmed completion of its latest round of strikes against Iran, with President Trump formally notifying Congress that hostilities have resumed. Iranian missiles reportedly struck oil tankers in the Strait of Hormuz overnight, killing at least one sailor, while Tehran claimed it struck a U.S. air base in Jordan — a sequence of events that has traders on edge ahead of the opening bell. Crude prices have surged to a four-week high, and gold is recovering from a two-week low as investors seek safety.

Monday's session offered a preview of how markets are digesting the conflict. Semiconductors bore the sharpest pain, falling more than five percent as a group, while Energy led all sectors with a gain of nearly one-and-a-half percent. Breadth was negative — 222 decliners against 170 advancers in our universe of 396 names — suggesting broad, if uneven, defensiveness. Refining names such as Valero Energy surged more than five percent, while memory and connectivity chip names suffered double-digit losses.

The day's pivotal data release will be the U.S. inflation print, which arrives as oil's escalation threatens to complicate the Federal Reserve's calculus. Watch energy equities at the open for follow-through, monitor the semiconductor complex for any stabilization after Monday's rout, and keep an eye on safe-haven flows in gold should headlines from the Strait of Hormuz intensify before the first trade.

briefs.headline: U.S. Strikes on Iran Concluded, Trump Alerts Congress
briefs.body: The White House formally notified lawmakers that a fresh round of military operations against Iran has been carried out, with the administration stating that targets included Iranian capabilities tied to the Strait of Hormuz. The notification signals an escalation in the conflict and raises the prospect of sustained disruption to one of the world's most critical oil chokepoints.

briefs.headline: Tankers Hit in the Strait, Shipping Risk Premiums Surge
briefs.body: Iran launched missiles that struck oil tankers transiting the Strait of Hormuz, according to the UAE, leaving one sailor dead. The incident immediately elevated war-risk insurance premiums and rattled energy traders who warn that dismissing the threat to Hormuz flows carries serious downside risk.

briefs.headline: Crude Jumps to Four-Week High as Supply Fears Mount
briefs.body: Oil prices reached their highest level in nearly a month as the U.S.-Iran confrontation widened. China's June crude imports had already fallen to a near decade-low amid the conflict, underscoring how sustained hostilities are reshaping global energy trade flows even before any formal supply blockade takes shape.

briefs.headline: Gold Rebounds Ahead of U.S. Inflation Data
briefs.body: Bullion recovered from a two-week trough in overnight trading as investors rotated toward traditional havens amid the Middle East escalation. The move comes just hours before the U.S. releases its latest consumer-price figures, a report that could either amplify or temper gold's defensive bid depending on whether inflation pressures are seen as broadening.

briefs.headline: Semiconductors Cratered Monday, Eyes on Any Stabilization at Open
briefs.body: The semiconductor sector shed more than five percent in Monday's session — the worst performance among all groups tracked — led by losses exceeding twelve percent in memory names and approaching eight percent in connectivity and IP chip stocks. Traders will be watching closely at Tuesday's open to determine whether the selloff finds support or accelerates on continued risk-off sentiment.

briefs.headline: Chipotle Charts International Expansion with Mexican Debut
briefs.body: The burrito chain is set to open its first location in Mexico, a symbolically significant move that tests whether its fast-casual model translates in the home country of its core cuisine. The expansion represents a milestone for the brand's international growth ambitions and adds a new variable for investors tracking its long-term unit-count trajectory.
